text.title: First 5 Paying Customers Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding First 5 Paying Customers Checklist. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Goal is exactly first 5 paid customers — not signups, not demos. Stop at 5 and switch to the conversion-loop methodology.
rule.rationale: Goal drift to 'signups' lets the founder feel busy without revenue.

rule.statement: Daily quota: ≥10 personalised outreach messages OR ≥1 published content asset, six days a week.
rule.rationale: Below quota the channel cannot warm up in 6 weeks.

rule.statement: Charge from day one — no free pilots, no 'invoice later'. First five must pay before access.
rule.rationale: Free pilots produce false signal and zero churn data.

rule.statement: One ICP segment only during the 6 weeks. Multi-ICP campaigns dilute message and outreach reuse.
rule.rationale: Two ICPs in 6 weeks = no message-market fit.

rule.statement: Weekly review: outreach sent, replies, demos, paid. Numbers logged or week is rerun.
Owner is named (solo founder counts). Co-founder ownership requires a single accountable name.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-first-5-paying-customers-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
